Title: High level accumulation of EPA and DHA in field-grown transgenic Camelina – a multi-territory evaluation of TAG accumulation and heterogeneity

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: omega-3 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid for fish-oil replacement and sustainable aquafeed/human nutrition applications
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ4 double bond into DPA to form DHA, likely on phosphatidylcholine-linked substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control point/terminal determinant for DHA accumulation in this engineered pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: construct swap/replacement in transgenic Camelina; field and glasshouse transgenic evaluation; GC-FID/GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes higher DHA accumulation and increased combined EPA+DHA content compared with alternative Δ4-desaturases
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Replacing the Emiliania huxleyi Δ4-desaturase with the Ostreococcus RCC809 Δ4-desaturase significantly improved pathway performance. 2. The DHA2015.1 construct containing O809D4 routinely produced combined EPA+DHA levels above 20% of total seed fatty acids, outperforming p7_DHA5 and B7.2. 3. The improved trait was stable across UK, USA and Canada field trials, with robust seed-specific accumulation of EPA and DHA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: seed-specific promoters; in DHA2015.1 the Δ4-desaturase cassette retained the same regulatory elements as prior construct
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field growth in UK, USA, Canada; seed developmental stage; premature harvest in USA_2017 reduced DHA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: preferred Δ4-desaturase choice for engineering DHA-rich Camelina; useful terminal-step gene for stacking with the established EPA/DHA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: associated within the multigene pathway with reduced seed oil content, reduced seed carbon and thousand grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies this enzyme swap as the only genetic change between constructs and attributes much of the improved DHA output to O809D4. Authors infer likely PC-linked substrate usage and note context-dependent enzyme performance in heterologous systems.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: omega-3 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid for fish-oil replacement and sustainable aquafeed/human nutrition applications
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ4 double bond into DPA to form DHA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant discussed; inferior to O809D4 in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative construct testing in transgenic Camelina; GC-FID metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports EPA/DHA accumulation but less effectively than O809D4-containing construct
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EhD4 in the p7_DHA5 construct supported accumulation of both EPA and DHA in Camelina seeds. 2. However, performance was inferior to the O809D4-containing DHA2015.1 construct, with benchmark combined EPA+DHA around 12–15% total fatty acids. 3. This comparison highlights the strong effect of terminal-step desaturase choice on pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: glasshouse and field comparisons in transgenic Camelina
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: serves as a functional but suboptimal Δ4-desaturase benchmark in EPA/DHA engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results section directly compares this Δ4-desaturase with O809D4 and TpD4 to identify the superior terminal-step enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: omega-3 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid for fish-oil replacement and sustainable aquafeed/human nutrition applications
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ4 double bond into DPA to form DHA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant discussed; less efficient than O809D4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative construct testing in transgenic Camelina; GC-FID metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: less efficient conversion of DPA to DHA, causing DPA accumulation and lower DHA than O809D4 construct
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The T. pseudonana Δ4-desaturase in construct B7.2 was less efficient at converting DPA to DHA. 2. This was indicated by greater DPA accumulation relative to p7_DHA5 and DHA2015.1. 3. The result helped identify O809D4 as the superior terminal-step gene for DHA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: not preferred for maximizing DHA because of poorer DPA-to-DHA conversion
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested as an alternative terminal enzyme and directly influenced the target metabolite profile.
